positions.title: Mentor
positions.description: I'm part of a team that teaches refugees HTML, Javscript, React and Node.js with the aim of giving them a foothold in the job market and improving integration. Aside from contributing to a political cause which I think is very important, teaching helps me consolidate my knowledge in web development.

positions.title: Front-end Developer & UX Designer
positions.description: In 2013 I traveled with Tigerspring on a 'workcation' adventure to Mauritius, where we combined building a software-startup with a tropical vacation. During the weekdays we coded from early morning till late evening and during weekends we went sight-seeing on the Island. The product was a game'ified platform with the purpose of crowdsourcing the tasks of an A&R manager. Every week Tigerspring would release exclusive content from future signings and users would have to guess which artists would be succesful. The product, which was called Wingzon, was not officially launched until 2016 and unfortunately it failed to gain the necessary traction in the market.
positions.label: case

portfolio.description: I have written a case about my work for Tigerspring Records.

positions.title: Instructor
positions.description: As an Instructor at CBS I was teaching exercise-focused classes in the following subjects: Human-Computer Interaction, Java programming, Distributed Systems. I put a lot of pride into preparing good exercises and trying to tailor and make them relevant for the students. And besides enjoying 'passing on' kowledge I learned how valuable it may be to teach something you have relatively recently learned yourself. Part-time position, 15-20 hours/week.
positions.label: case
